text.title: Microservices Service Boundaries Examples
text.summary: Worked example: decompose monolith into order/billing/inventory boundaries.

example.context: 5-year-old Rails monolith; 200k LoC; 1 DB with 80 tables; 3 engineering teams. Need to split for independent deploys.
example.walk: Step 1: event-storming → bounded contexts = {Order, Billing, Inventory, CustomerProfile, Notification}. Step 2: orders/order_items → Order; charges/refunds → Billing; stock_levels → Inventory; users/addresses → CustomerProfile; emails_sent → Notification. Cross-context joins identified: order.user_id → customer_profile (15 joins) → flagged for replacement with CDC-replicated profile snapshot. Step 3: checkout-team owns Order + Notification; payments-team owns Billing; fulfillment-team owns Inventory + CustomerProfile. Step 4: each service publishes OpenAPI in schema-registry/{service}/v1. Step 5: ADR-101 per service committed; cross-team review approves.
example.output: 5 services; 0 shared DBs; 0 cross-service joins; each team deploys independently; 30% deploy frequency increase.
